easy.q: What is the first book of the Bible?
What is the last book of the Bible?
Who built the ark?
Where was Jesus born?
Who was swallowed by a great fish?
How many disciples did Jesus choose?
Who betrayed Jesus?
Who baptized Jesus?
Who was the mother of Jesus?
Who denied Jesus three times?
Who was the first man?
Who was the first woman?
What did Jesus turn water into?
Who killed Goliath?
"The Lord is my ___" (Psalm 23)
What sign did God give Noah after the flood?
On which day did God rest?
Who led Israel out of Egypt?
Who was thrown into the lions' den?
What did Jesus ride into Jerusalem?
How many days did it rain in the flood?
What was the garden where Adam and Eve lived?

med.q: How many books are in the Protestant Bible?
How many plagues struck Egypt?
Which sea did Moses part?
On which mountain were the Ten Commandments given?
Who interpreted Pharaoh's dreams?
Who was the wisest king of Israel?
Who was the first king of Israel?
Whose strength left him when his hair was cut?
Which prophet was taken up in a whirlwind?
Which apostle had been a tax collector?
Where were believers first called "Christians"?
Who wrote most of the New Testament letters?
On the road to where was Saul converted?
Who climbed a tree to see Jesus?
How many people were saved on Noah's ark?
How many loaves fed the five thousand?
Which disciple doubted the resurrection?
Which queen saved her people from Haman?
How many days was Lazarus dead before being raised?
What food did God provide in the wilderness?
Who was renamed from Abram?
What was Abraham's promised son's name?
Who walked on water then began to sink?
Who was Ruth's mother-in-law?
Who tried to wash his hands of Jesus' death?

hard.q: What is the shortest verse in the Bible (KJV)?
What was Paul's trade?
Which three were thrown into the fiery furnace?
Which book has the most chapters?
Who was the first Christian martyr?
How many fruits of the Spirit are listed (Gal 5)?
Who is called "a man after God's own heart"?
What did the dove bring back to Noah?
Who succeeded Moses to lead Israel?
How many books are in the Old Testament?
Which prophet was told to marry Gomer?
Who rebuilt Jerusalem's walls as governor?
In which garden did Jesus pray before His arrest?
Whose ear did Peter cut off at Jesus' arrest?
Who was the father of John the Baptist?
How many years did the Israelites wander in the wilderness?
Which Gospel was written by a physician?
What was the name of Abraham's wife?
In what city was Paul imprisoned when he wrote several epistles?
Who was the oldest man recorded in the Bible?

edifying.q: According to Philippians 4:6-7, what guards our hearts when we pray with thanksgiving instead of worrying?
edifying.n: The peace of God, beyond understanding, guards your heart and mind.
edifying.r: Philippians 4:6-7 — bring it to God before you carry it alone.

edifying.q: What did Jesus call the greatest commandment?
o: Love the Lord your God with all your heart
edifying.n: Love God fully — and your neighbour as yourself.

edifying.q: According to Micah 6:8, what does the Lord require of us?
o: Act justly, love mercy, walk humbly
edifying.n: Justice, mercy, and humility before God.
edifying.r: Micah 6:8 — a whole life in one verse.

edifying.q: In Galatians 5, the fruit of the Spirit begins with which quality?
edifying.n: Love — then joy, peace, patience, and the rest.

edifying.q: Proverbs 3:5 tells us to trust in the Lord and not lean on our own ___.
edifying.n: Trust Him fully; He makes your paths straight.

edifying.q: According to Romans 12:2, we are transformed by the renewing of our ___.
edifying.n: A renewed mind reshapes how you live.

edifying.q: Matthew 6:33 says to seek first the ___ of God.
edifying.n: Seek His kingdom first; the rest is added.

edifying.q: According to John 13:35, how will everyone know we are Jesus' disciples?
o: By our love for one another
edifying.n: Love is the mark of a disciple.

edifying.q: James 1:19 says to be quick to listen, slow to speak, and slow to ___.
edifying.n: Listen first; anger last.

edifying.q: Psalm 119:105 calls God's word a lamp to my feet and a ___ to my path.
edifying.n: The Word lights the next step.

edifying.q: In 1 Corinthians 13, which remains the greatest?
edifying.n: Faith, hope and love remain — the greatest is love.

edifying.q: Ephesians 2:8-9 says we are saved by grace through ___, not by works.
edifying.n: Grace is a gift, never a wage.

edifying.q: Isaiah 40:31 says those who hope in the Lord will renew their strength and soar on wings like ___.
edifying.n: Wait on Him and rise renewed.

edifying.q: In the Beatitudes, the peacemakers are called the ___ of God.
edifying.n: Blessed are the peacemakers.

edifying.q: Joshua 1:9 commands us to be strong and ___, for the Lord is with us.
edifying.n: Courage flows from His presence.

edifying.q: Romans 8:28 says all things work together for ___ to those who love God.
edifying.n: Even hard things are woven for good.

edifying.q: Matthew 11:28 — "Come to me, all you who are weary, and I will give you ___."
edifying.n: Bring the weight to Him; receive rest.

edifying.q: 2 Timothy 1:7 — God gave us a spirit not of fear, but of power, love and a sound ___.
edifying.n: Power, love, self-discipline — not fear.

edifying.q: Hebrews 11:1 — faith is the substance of things hoped for, the evidence of things not ___.
edifying.n: Faith sees what eyes cannot yet.

edifying.q: 1 John 1:9 — if we confess our sins, He is faithful and just to ___ us.
edifying.n: Confession meets faithful forgiveness.

edifying.q: 1 Thessalonians 5:16-18 — rejoice always, pray continually, and give ___ in all circumstances.
edifying.n: Gratitude in everything is God's will for you.

edifying.q: Psalm 46:10 — "Be still, and know that I am ___."
edifying.n: Stillness is where we know Him.

edifying.q: Galatians 6:9 — let us not grow weary in doing ___, for we will reap a harvest.
edifying.n: Keep sowing good; the harvest comes.

edifying.q: Romans 5:8 — while we were still ___, Christ died for us.
edifying.n: Loved before we deserved it.

edifying.q: Colossians 3:23 — whatever you do, work at it with all your heart, as working for the ___.
edifying.n: Ordinary work becomes worship.

edifying.q: Matthew 6:14 — if you forgive others, your heavenly Father will also ___ you.
edifying.n: Forgiveness given opens forgiveness received.

edifying.q: Philippians 4:13 — "I can do all things through ___ who strengthens me."
edifying.n: Strength for all things is found in Him.

edifying.q: Proverbs 17:17 — a ___ loves at all times.
edifying.n: Steady love marks true friendship.

edifying.q: John 14:6 — Jesus said, "I am the way, the ___, and the life."
edifying.n: The way, the truth, the life.

edifying.q: Lamentations 3:23 — His mercies are new every ___.
edifying.n: Fresh mercy waits each morning.
